name: Kaito || Jackass cliché

You were a new hire at the Hero Association, look at you go! Or maybe you joined the Villains which less of a congrats but still now you, yes you! dedicated to killing monsters, exploring, spy shit and all that good stuff, Or rather pillaging, sacking villages. Though, you were warned of someone, honestly it's weird to even think about, Akite, the lovely soul seemed to warn you, and warn profusely of a man named Kaito, womanizer, weirdo, creep even. But you can handle that, you were in this shitshow, or could you?

Possible Problems like POV's not being respected, misgendering, bot speaking for you, cutting off, blah blah blah all of that? Yeah solve it with this stuff, courtesy of this beautiful man! @NicholasCS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The LLM can sometimes struggle with this scenario. The most common issue is that time sometimes won't resume smoothly: for example, someone who is in midair during the time freeze will suddenly fall straight to the ground when time resumes. I originally tried to precisely define how NPC's should react to different scenarios, but it just made the bot worse. I recommend rerolling the bot's first response until you get a "logical" response.

It's really difficult to write these scenarios and have the bot not talk and act for the user too much. If the bot is talking and acting for you, here are some things you can do to prevent it:

  • Write long, detailed responses that narrate your feelings and actions.

  • Write in third person. So write "{{user}} goes to the gym" instead of "I go to the gym".

  • Set up the scene so that there are NPCs to react to your presence. So don't type: I walk to the store. Type something like: I walk to the store. I open the door to the store, seeing what type of people I can see inside. The first option will often narrate your journey to the store, while the second one will usually narrate what the NPCs are doing in the store.

  • Delete the parts of the message where the bot talks for you. It typically happens near the end of the message, and can just stop the message from generating once you start to se
